reasoning: The requirements describe a standard CRUD application, for which a simple monolithic architecture with a separate frontend and backend is a well-established and efficient pattern. The chosen tech stack (React, FastAPI, SQLite) is modern, widely supported, and ideal for rapid development of such an application. SQLite fulfills the persistence requirement without the overhead of a dedicated database server, which is appropriate for a single-user application. The tasks are clearly divisible and can be worked on in parallel by the respective roles.

architecture.high_level_design: A single-page application (SPA) built with React consumes a RESTful backend API. The backend, built with FastAPI and Python, handles all business logic for CRUD operations on todo items. Data is persisted in a local SQLite database file. The entire application (frontend and backend) will be containerized using Docker for development and deployment consistency.

task_split.designer: Create low-fidelity wireframes for the todo application. The design must include: 1. The main list view for all todos. 2. The input form for adding a new todo (with 'title' and 'description' fields). 3. The visual states for a single todo item: default, completed (e.g., with a strikethrough), and hover (to show the delete button).
task_split.fe_dev: Implement the React frontend application based on the designer's wireframes and the defined API contract. Create reusable components for the todo list, individual todo items, and the 'add todo' form. Implement logic to fetch, create, update (toggle completion), and delete todos by making asynchronous calls to the backend API.
task_split.be_dev: Develop the backend REST API using FastAPI. Implement the data model for a todo item (id, title, description, completed). Create all CRUD endpoints as specified in the API contract. Use SQLAlchemy or the native sqlite3 library to connect to and manage the SQLite database for data persistence.

endpoints.description: Retrieve the list of all todo items.
Update an existing todo item, primarily to toggle its completion status.
Delete a todo item by its ID.
